channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: The car is abandon in the block of flats, 28th June
item.description:
The car is left in a block of flats 41-83 Morvale Close. The car is left there for months there as not tax or insurance .registration VL98 DKA

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Another dumping at our hotspot beaide house

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Riverdale Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London

item.title: Fly tip, 28th June
item.description:
Sidcup by pass as you turn at bottom of west hallowes

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sidcup Road, Eltham

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 340A, East Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London

item.title: Rubbish on the side of the road, 28th June
item.description:
This has been an ongoing issue that we have tried to resolve.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cornwall Drive, Greater London

item.title: Please stop leaving bags here, 28th June
item.description:
Every other day this occurs, please take bags away when you empty the bin? Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Monnow Road, Bermondsey, Greater London

item.title: Blocking access to pavementI’, 28th June
item.description:
As you can see, this vehicle is blocking the access 2 and from the zebra crossing. It needs to be removed ASAP

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nightingale Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees overgrown, 28th June
item.description:
Few Trees need to be pruned as they are blocking the street lighting making it a dark road to walk through. There are so many low branches hanging down which are now hanging onto the road hitting vehicles as they pass.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Crescent, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light out, 28th June
item.description:
The street light has been out few days. Also this is Making it very dark and gloomy as most of the trees on the street have overgrown over the road so no lights are actually lighting up the road. Having this light not working is making it very dark to walk.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Crescent, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bike, 28th June
item.description:
Against wall nr St Joseph's church

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Plaistow Lane, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lants dying, stolen and unmaintained in communal garden, 28th June
item.description:
The garden here has been in poor condition for several weeks. A number of plants appear to have died, likely due to a lack of regular watering and maintenance, and some have been removed or stolen. The beds look bare and neglected
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Thrasher Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead tree on Clarissa Street, 28th June
item.description:
There is a dead tree on the pavement on Clarissa Street. The tree appears to have dried out completely and has no living foliage. As a dead street tree it may pose a safety risk and should be assessed for removal and replacement as soon as possible.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Clarissa Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Excessive rubbish accumulation on Swimmers Lane, 28th June
item.description:
There is a significant build-up of rubbish on Swimmers Lane, E8. This is a public pathway and the current state makes it unpleasant and potentially hazardous for kids, dogs, and wild life
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Swimmers Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deceased fox, 28th June
item.description:
Two foxes were hit by a car then moved to the pavement. One was still alive and rushed to Vets Now in Sidcup and the other one who was already dead remains here. So there is just one fox, not two.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eversley Avenue, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car parking on my drive without permission, 28th June
item.description:
I am an old age pensioner . Someone keeps parking on and across my drive. This is stopping my career's from parking at my property. The vehicle is a Mercedes-Benz. Please help me to get sorted. Im unable to get to my door . The vehicle parks on my drive and or across every day. 17 curan avenue Kind regards
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Curran Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bikes dumped, 27th June
item.description:
Several lime bikes have been dumped on my estate
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Park Hill, Clapham, Greater London

item.title: Why are yellow lines only 11-12, 27th June

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Camden Park Road, Elmstead, Greater London

item.title: When existing Lubbock road you can not see what is coming down the road, 27th June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Camden Park Road, Elmstead, Greater London

item.title: Children's play area full of dumped dockless bikes, 27th June
item.description:
People are parking various dockless bikes in the playground
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Drummond Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A Masterclass in Selfish Parking, 27th June
item.description:
To whichever parking enforcement officer is covering this area, please take a look at the attached photos of a white Nissan Micra (Registration: DS11 UYA). Every single week, from Friday through to Monday, this vehicle is abandoned with an almost impressive lack of consideration for anyone else. The driver has masterfully managed to park in a way that leaves just over half a car’s length of completely dead, unusable space either in front of or behind them. In a neighborhood where parking is at an absolute premium, this driver apparently believes they are entitled to a double-wide driveway. By failing to do something as basic as pulling forward or backing up properly, they successfully kill an entire extra parking space for the whole weekend while their car sits completely stationary. Since this spectacular display of inconsiderate parking is a highly predictable, weekly routine, it would be wonderful if enforcement could schedule a weekend visit to reward their creativity with a penalty charge notice. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Montrell Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
